    Sprint 0 – Problem Framing, Infrastructure Setup & RAG Foundations

    📄

    Tooling overview (LangGraph, vector DBs, LLM APIs, etc.)

    Lesson 2

    In this lesson, we'll outline the core tooling and frameworks we'll utilize throughout the bootcamp. Familiarizing yourself with these tools will enhance your productivity and effectiveness when working on AI engineering projects.


    Agentic Workflows and Multi-Agent Systems


    • LangGraph: Framework for orchestrating agentic workflows. GitHub

    • ADK: Powerful orchestrator for multi-agent systems. GitHub


    Prompt Optimization and Output Management


    • Instructor: Python library for structured output handling. Website


    Data Storage and Retrieval


    • Qdrant: High-performance Vector Database. Website


    API Management and Guardrails


    • LiteLLM: Unified router and proxy for LLM APIs, providing guardrail capabilities and fallbacks. Website

    • GuardrailsAI: Implements guardrails for LLM inputs and outputs. GitHub


    Observability and Evaluation


    • LangSmith: Platform for LLM observability and evaluation. Website


    UI Prototyping


    • Streamlit: Python library for rapid UI development. Website


    Agent Communication Protocols


    • MCP: Context retrieval standard for agentic applications developed by Anthropic.

    • A2A: Cross-agent communication protocol by Google, enabling IoA (Internet of Agents).


    Infrastructure and Deployment


    • Docker: Containerization platform for consistent deployment. Website

    • Docker Compose: Tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications. Docs

    • Kubernetes: Container orchestration platform. Website


    AWS Services


    • EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service): Managed Kubernetes service by AWS.

    • ECR (Elastic Container Registry): AWS-managed container registry service.


    Version Control and CI/CD


    • GitHub and GitHub Actions: Repository hosting and continuous integration/deployment platform. GitHub


    LLM APIs


    • OpenAI: OpenAI Platform

    • Google: Google AI

    • Groq: Groq Console


    What's Next?


    If you are familiar with some of these tools, fantastic! If not, that's fine - we will thoroughly cover each tool as we progress through the bootcamp. This week, ensure your development environment (Lesson 5) is fully set up, and configure your LLM API keys (Lesson 6).


    Agentic AI Infrastructure in Context


    To better understand the role of each tool we will explore, it's helpful to view them through the lens of the Enterprise Agentic AI Hierarchy of Needs a framework I recently shared in my newsletter that outlines the layered infrastructure required for production‑grade agentic systems


    Why Observability & Evaluation Comes First


    In enterprise settings, non-determinism from LLM chains can break systems silently. Prioritizing instumentation, prompt versioning, and evaluation ensures you have instrumentation and feedback loops before scaling or introducing complex orchestration.


    Security, Memory & Communication


    As systems progress toward GA, it's critical to embed security guardrails (GuardrailsAI, LiteLLM) and establish structured memory + protocol layers (Instructor for structured outputs, MCP/A2A standards). These layers are cornerstones of enterprise readiness.



    *You can read more in SwirlAI newsletter: https://www.newsletter.swirlai.com/p/enterprise-agentic-ai-hierarchy-of
